etymology.description: Depicts a tool—chisel or awl—used to mark slaves and criminals -> 辛
etymology.interpretation: The character 辛 (xīn) originally depicted a tool such as a chisel, used for marking or branding. The association with a sharp tool helps explain its extended meaning of 'spice,' as spices can have a pungent, sharp quality reminiscent of a tool's point or effect.

examples.chinese: 这个菜有一种辛辣的味道(這個菜有一種辛辣的味道)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège cài yǒu yī zhǒng xīnlà de wèidào.
examples.english: This dish has a spicy taste.

examples.chinese: 他喜欢在食物里添加辛香料(他喜歡在食物裡添加辛香料)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān zài shíwù lǐ tiānjiā xīn xiāngliào.
examples.english: He likes to add spices to the food.

examples.chinese: 这种辛口的调料非常受欢迎(這種辛口的調料非常受歡迎)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè zhǒng xīnkǒu de tiáoliào fēicháng shòu huānyíng.
examples.english: This type of spicy seasoning is very popular.
